claim: A 2021 Lancet Planetary Health study estimated wildfire smoke contributes up to 25 percent of global ambient PM2.5, with smoke PM2.5 potentially 2-3x more toxic per unit.

source.title: Global estimates of mortality associated with long-term exposure to wildfire-related PM2.5

method: Reviewed smoke-exposure estimation methodology, health-impact modeling, and toxicity analysis.
limitations: Smoke-exposure estimates rely on satellite AOD which has cloud-cover gaps.
Differential toxicity finding requires further replication.
